items.docs:
Creates or Updates the Alarm. When creating alarm, platform generates Alarm Id as [time-based UUID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Version_1_(date-time_and_MAC_address)). The newly created Alarm id will be present in the response. Specify existing Alarm id to update the alarm. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause 'Not Found' error. 

Platform also deduplicate the alarms based on the entity id of originator and alarm 'type'. For example, if the user or system

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Clear the Alarm. Once cleared, the 'clear_ts' field will be set to current timestamp and special rule chain event 'ALARM_CLEAR' will be generated. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

params.name: assigneeId
params.description: A string value representing the user id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Assign the Alarm. Once assigned, the 'assign_ts' field will be set to current timestamp and special rule chain event 'ALARM_ASSIGNED' (or ALARM_REASSIGNED in case of assigning already assigned alarm) will be generated. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Acknowledge the Alarm. Once acknowledged, the 'ack_ts' field will be set to current timestamp and special rule chain event 'ALARM_ACK' will be generated. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: statusList
params.description: A list of string values separated by comma ',' representing one of the AlarmSearchStatus enumeration value

params.name: severityList
params.description: A list of string values separated by comma ',' representing one of the AlarmSeverity enumeration value

params.name: typeList
params.description: A list of string values separated by comma ',' representing alarm types

params.name: assigneeId
params.description: A string value representing the assignee user id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Maximum amount of entities in a one page

params.name: page
params.description: Sequence number of page starting from 0

params.name: textSearch
params.description: The case insensitive 'substring' filter based on of next alarm fields: type, severity or status

params.name: sortProperty
params.description: Property of entity to sort by

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: Sort order. ASC (ASCENDING) or DESC (DESCENDING)

params.name: startTime
params.description: The start timestamp in milliseconds of the search time range over the Alarm class field: 'createdTime'.

params.name: endTime
params.description: The end timestamp in milliseconds of the search time range over the Alarm class field: 'createdTime'.

items.docs: Returns a page of alarms that belongs to the current user owner. If the user has the authority of 'Tenant Administrator', the server returns alarms that belongs to the tenant of current user. If the user has the authority of 'Customer User', the server returns alarms that belongs to the customer of current user. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more det

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

Returns a page of alarms for the selected entity. Specifying both parameters 'searchStatus' and 'status' at the same time will cause an error. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs: Fetch the Alarm object based on the provided Alarm Id. If the user has the authority of 'Tenant Administrator', the server checks that the originator of alarm is owned by the same tenant. If the user has the authority of 'Customer User', the server checks that the originator of alarm belongs to the customer.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Deletes the Alarm. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

items.docs: Returns a set of unique alarm types based on alarms that are either owned by the tenant or assigned to the customer which user is performing the request.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Fetch the Alarm Info object based on the provided Alarm Id. If the user has the authority of 'Tenant Administrator', the server checks that the originator of alarm is owned by the same tenant. If the user has the authority of 'Customer User', the server checks that the originator of alarm belongs to the customer. Alarm Info is an extension of the default Alarm object that also contains name of the alarm originator.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: assigneeId
params.description: A string value representing the assignee user id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Search the alarms by originator ('entityType' and entityId') and optional 'status' or 'searchStatus' filters and returns the highest AlarmSeverity(CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, WARNING or INDETERMINATE). Specifying both parameters 'searchStatus' and 'status' at the same time will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Unassign the Alarm. Once unassigned, the 'assign_ts' field will be set to current timestamp and special rule chain event 'ALARM_UNASSIGNED' will be generated. Referencing non-existing Alarm Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

items.docs:
Returns a page of alarm comments for specified alarm. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Creates or Updates the Alarm Comment. When creating comment, platform generates Alarm Comment Id as [time-based UUID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Version_1_(date-time_and_MAC_address)). The newly created Alarm Comment id will be present in the response. Specify existing Alarm Comment id to update the alarm. Referencing non-existing Alarm Comment Id will cause 'Not Found' error. 

 To create new Alarm comment entity it is enough to specify 'comment' json element wi

params.name: alarmId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

params.name: commentId
params.description: A string value representing the alarm comment id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Deletes the Alarm comment. Referencing non-existing Alarm comment Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' or 'CUSTOMER_USER' authority.
